summary: Four files reviewed. The visual shell is premium but the copy has three trust-killing patterns and one pricing page friction point that are actively leaking conversion: (1) public stats expose 'drafted' and 'staged' counts, signaling an incomplete product; (2) urgency copy on both exam pages reads like a crypto presale ('founding buyers', 'build is accelerating', 'bank is still compounding'); (3) the upgrade page has no annual savings dollar callout and one feature bullet ('More aggressive targeting') that is meaningless to a nurse; (4) the home page featured-intent-links contain internal routing labels as visible span copy ('Direct comparison traffic', 'Tutor-intent buyer traffic') that will render on the live page.
recommendations: {"priority":1,"surface":"ccrn/page.tsx + nclex/page.tsx stats props","issue":"stats expose \u0027draft\u0027 counts (\u0027X staged questions\u0027, \u0027X original NCLEX questions drafted\u0027). Visitors read this as \u0027the product is not ready.\u0027","fix":"Show only live counts. If NCLEX live count is low, replace the second stat with a capability signal: { label: \u0027Review style\u0027, value: \u0027Priority + safety first\u0027 }. Remove any stat value containing the word \u0027drafted\u0027 or \u0027staged\u0027 from public props.","effort":"15 min"}

{"priority":2,"surface":"ccrn/page.tsx urgencyTitle/Body + nclex/page.tsx urgencyTitle/Body","issue":"Both pages use founding-era presale framing: \u0027Get in while the bank is still compounding\u0027, \u0027Founding buyers get the cleaner package now\u0027, \u0027Get in early while the NCLEX build is accelerating\u0027, \u0027before the broader draft bank...fully mature\u0027. This telegraphs an unfinished product and undermines the premium positioning everywhere else.","fix":"Replace with outcome-anchored urgency. CCRN: title=\u0027Your CCRN window is closer than it feels.\u0027, body=\u0027Every shift you study without a sharper clinical rationale is a shift you can\u0027t get back. Start with the critical-care questions that actually move pattern recognition.\u0027 NCLEX: title=\u0027Most repeat test-takers wait too long to change what they study.\u0027, body=\u0027ChapAI NCLEX reorders the noise into a cleaner safety-first sequence before your next window.\u0027","effort":"20 min"}

{"priority":3,"surface":"upgrade/page.tsx — billing toggle + Pro NCLEX feature bullet","issue":"The annual/monthly toggle shows no savings amount, leaving the biggest conversion lever unused. Pro NCLEX feature \u0027More aggressive targeting\u0027 is internal jargon — a nurse has no idea what it means.","fix":"Add a savings callout inside the toggle label: \u0027Annual · Save $58\u0027 (calculated as (29-24)*12). Change Pro NCLEX feature to \u0027Smarter weak-area targeting\u0027 or \u0027Focused gap sequencing\u0027. Both are one-line changes with zero layout risk.","effort":"15 min"}

{"priority":4,"surface":"page.tsx (home) — featured-intent-links span copy","issue":"Each featured-intent-link has a \u003cspan\u003e child that reads like a dev/SEO note: \u0027Direct comparison traffic.\u0027, \u0027Tutor-intent buyer traffic.\u0027, \u0027Structure-first buyer intent.\u0027 These render visibly to users and destroy premium feel instantly.","fix":"Replace with user-facing benefit copy. \u0027ChapAI vs Archer\u0027 → span: \u0027See how the question depth compares.\u0027 | \u0027CCRN AI tutor\u0027 → span: \u0027Get a rationale after every answer.\u0027 | \u0027NCLEX study plan\u0027 → span: \u0027Know exactly what to study next.\u0027 | \u0027CCRN hemodynamics questions\u0027 → span: \u0027Shock, preload, and perfusion drills.\u0027","effort":"10 min"}

durable_memory: Never expose draft/staged question counts on public exam landing pages — always show live counts only or capability signals
Founding-era urgency copy ('get in early', 'bank is compounding') signals incomplete product and must be replaced with outcome-anchored urgency before scaling paid traffic
Annual billing toggle must display dollar savings amount — highest-leverage pricing page change with near-zero effort
All featured-intent-link span copy must be user-facing benefit language, never internal SEO routing labels
active_context: Home page: featured-intent-link spans contain internal labels — live bug, visible to users now
NCLEX stat prop passes summary.nclex.draft (not live) — verify live count before deciding on stat replacement
Upgrade Pro NCLEX feature 'More aggressive targeting' is jargon — change to plain benefit language
Annual savings not shown on toggle — add 'Save $58' label to annual option

current_blocker: The home page featured-intent-link span copy ('Direct comparison traffic', 'Tutor-intent buyer traffic', 'Structure-first buyer intent') is rendering live to visitors right now and is the most urgent trust-killer. Fix this before any paid traffic or SEO traffic scales.
suggested_human_fix: In page.tsx lines 136-149, update each featured-intent-link span to user-facing copy (see recommendation 4 above). Then open ccrn/page.tsx and nclex/page.tsx and remove any stat value that contains the word 'drafted' or 'staged'. These two changes are under 30 minutes combined and remove the two highest-visibility trust leaks.
next_task: Implement the four copy fixes in order of priority, then do a single live pass on /upgrade with the annual toggle active to confirm the savings callout renders and the Pro NCLEX feature bullet is updated. No design or layout changes required — all four fixes are prop/string changes only.
